introGreenProduction.title: Introduction to Green Production
introGreenProduction.description: A practical, hands-on course for film and TV professionals focused on sustainable production practices. Learn to produce differently, minimizing environmental impact while maintaining creativity, from set selection to crew transport, energy supply, and material sourcing.

masteringGreenProduction.title: Mastering Green Production
masteringGreenProduction.description: Addressing urgent environmental priorities in filmmaking: key sustainability challenges, international regulations, and strategies for reducing production impact while accessing green funding opportunities, tailored for producers, film commissions, financiers, and broadcasters.

carbonFootprinting.title: Carbon Calculation for the Film and TV Industry
carbonFootprinting.description: Learn to measure carbon footprints in film and TV production. Covers climate issues, carbon assessment methodologies, data handling, result interpretation, sustainability labels, and carbon offsetting, with interactive exercises and case studies.

greenAnimation.title: Green Animation
greenAnimation.description: Sustainable animation production for studios: understand the structural impacts of energy use, digital infrastructure, equipment, mobility, and everyday practices, and learn to prioritize actions and build a realistic, effective sustainability approach without compromising creative quality or production efficiency.

about.initiativeBanner: StepUP is a Eurimages initiative developed by Ecoprod.

partners.title: Our Partners
partners.subtitle: StepUP is supported by a network of partners committed to sustainable audiovisual production across Europe.

items.bftp: The Bæredygtig Film og TV Produktion (bftp) is a Danish alliance dedicated to reducing the environmental impact of audiovisual productions by providing guidance and resources for more sustainable practices.
items.bgreen: B.Green is a Czech initiative promoting sustainability in film production by reducing carbon footprints and prioritizing local, renewable resources.
items.ecomuvi: EcoMuvi, accredited by ACCREDIA, sets an international standard for sustainability in audiovisual productions. From documentaries to fiction, it ensures top environmental, social, and economic standards in every project.
items.green-eyes: Green Eyes is a sustainability consultancy based in Hungary and the UK for the film and TV industry, supporting productions with green policies, on-set coordination, and carbon reduction strategies.
items.green-producers-club: The Green Producers Club supports the creative industries with knowledge sharing (The Club) and impact measurement (The Tool), helping producers plan, track, and reduce emissions across productions.
items.green-filming-cz: Greenfilming.cz is a Czech platform offering tools, practical advice, and a database of eco-friendly suppliers to promote sustainable practices in audiovisual production.
items.green-film-shooting: Green Film Shooting informs creative professionals about green measures, eco-friendly approaches and innovative solutions for film and media production.
items.on-tourne-vert: On Tourne Vert (Rolling Green) is a Quebec-based association supporting the audiovisual industry's green transition through tools, resources, and sustainable production accreditation.
items.films4future: Films for Future is a Dutch collective promoting sustainability in film and TV production through industry collaboration and eco-friendly practices.
items.wallimage: Wallimage is a Belgian public fund that supports Wallonia's audiovisual industry through project co-financing, business investment, and funding for film, TV, and video game productions.
items.annee-helvig-frost: Annee Helvig Frost is a sustainability expert within the Danish / Nordic film & TV industry, and co-founder of the Nordic Eco Media Alliance (NEMA).
items.valentina-huckova: Valentína Hučková is a certified Green Filming coordinator and consultant focused on sustainable film production, carbon reduction, and education through workshops on eco-friendly art and media.
items.dorte-schneider-garcia: Dörte Schneider Garcia, a German assistant director based in Portugal, is a certified green consultant promoting sustainable film production and industry training.

footer.ecoprodDesc: A collective initiative supporting the ecological transition of the audiovisual and film industry.
footer.eurimagesDesc: The Council of Europe's fund for co-production, distribution, and exhibition of European cinematographic works.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.acceptanceText: By accessing and using the StepUP platform, you accept and agree to be bound by these Terms of Use. If you do not agree with any part of these terms, you must not use this platform.
terms.usageText: StepUP is an educational platform dedicated to sustainable audiovisual production. You agree to use this platform only for lawful purposes and in accordance with these terms. You must not misuse the platform or attempt to access it using a method other than the interface provided.
terms.ipText: All content on the StepUP platform, including but not limited to courses, webinars, videos, text, graphics, and software, is the property of StepUP, Eurimages, Ecoprod, or their respective licensors. This content is protected by copyright and other intellectual property laws.
terms.liabilityText: StepUP is provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis. We do not guarantee that the platform will be uninterrupted, secure, or error-free. To the fullest extent permitted by law, StepUP shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages.
terms.modificationsText: We reserve the right to modify these Terms of Use at any time. Changes will be effective immediately upon posting on the platform. Your continued use of StepUP after any changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.
terms.contactText: For any questions regarding these Terms of Use, please contact us at contact@stepup-platform.eu.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.collectionText: We collect personal information that you voluntarily provide when registering, enrolling in courses, or contacting us. This may include your name, email address, and learning progress data.
privacy.useText: Your personal data is used to provide and improve our services, manage your account, track your learning progress, and communicate with you about courses and updates. We do not sell your personal information to third parties.
privacy.cookiesText: StepUP uses essential cookies to ensure the proper functioning of the platform. Analytics cookies may be used to understand how the platform is used and to improve the user experience. You can manage your cookie preferences through your browser settings.
privacy.rightsText: In accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), you have the right to access, rectify, delete, and port your personal data. You may also object to the processing of your data or request restriction of processing. To exercise these rights, contact us at privacy@stepup-platform.eu.
privacy.contactText: For any questions regarding this Privacy Policy or data protection, please contact our Data Protection Officer at privacy@stepup-platform.eu.

notice.title: Legal Notice
notice.intro: In accordance with Articles 6-III and 19 of French Law no. 2004-575 of 21 June 2004 on Confidence in the Digital Economy (LCEN), users of the StepUP website and platform are informed of the identity of the parties involved in its creation and operation.
notice.editorStatusValue: Non-profit association governed by the French law of 1 July 1901
notice.publicationText: The publication director is Pervenche Beurier, Managing Director of the Ecoprod association.
notice.hostingText: The site is currently hosted by GitHub, Inc., 88 Colin P. Kelly Jr Street, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA. This information will be updated if the site migrates to another host.
notice.ipText: All content on the StepUP platform (e-learning courses, webinars, videos, text, graphics, logos and software) is protected by intellectual property law and remains the property of the Ecoprod association, Eurimages or their respective licensors. Any reproduction, representation or distribution, in whole or in part, without prior written authorisation is prohibited.
notice.dataText: The processing of your personal data is described in our Privacy Policy, accessible from the site footer.
notice.contactText: For any question regarding the site or this legal notice, please write to us at stepup@ecoprod.com.

sales.title: Terms of Sale
sales.objectText: These terms of sale govern the sale of online (e-learning) courses offered by the Ecoprod association through the StepUP platform. Any order of a paid course implies the user's full acceptance of these terms.
sales.servicesText: StepUP offers online training modules in SCORM format, accessible remotely from a web browser after creating an account. Some courses are free, others are paid. The description, estimated duration and content of each course are specified on its page.
sales.priceText: Prices are stated in euros. The price that applies is the one displayed on the course page at the time of the order. Ecoprod reserves the right to change its prices at any time, it being understood that the course is billed at the rate in force when the order is confirmed.
sales.orderText: The order is confirmed after selecting the course, accepting these terms and paying online. Payment is made by credit card via our secure payment provider Stripe. Ecoprod does not have access to the user's banking details. The order is confirmed by a summary e-mail.
sales.accessText: Access to the course opens immediately after payment confirmation, from the user's dashboard. Access is strictly personal: login credentials must not be shared. The user's progress and results are saved to allow the course to be resumed and a certificate of completion to be issued.
sales.withdrawalText: Under Article L221-18 of the French Consumer Code, the consumer normally has 14 days to exercise a right of withdrawal. However, as this is digital content supplied on an intangible medium whose performance begins immediately, the user expressly acknowledges, when confirming the order, waiving the right of withdrawal as soon as access to the course begins, in accordance with Article L221-28 13° of the French Consumer Code. No refund may be claimed once access to the course has been opened.
sales.durationText: Unless otherwise stated on the course page, access to a purchased course is guaranteed for a minimum period specified at the time of purchase, from payment confirmation. Ecoprod strives to keep the platform available but cannot be held liable for temporary unavailability due to maintenance or force majeure.
sales.certificateText: On completing a course under the required conditions (in particular the required completion rate), a certificate of completion may be issued to the user. This certificate attests solely to the completion of the course. It does not constitute a diploma, a qualification, or a state-recognised professional certification. StepUP courses are not Qualiopi-certified and are not registered with the French national register of professional certifications (RNCP) or the specific register (RS). As such, they are not eligible for vocational training funding (CPF, OPCO, etc.), unless expressly stated otherwise on the course page.
sales.claimsText: Any complaint may be sent to stepup@ecoprod.com. Under Articles L612-1 et seq. of the French Consumer Code, consumers have the right to use a consumer mediator free of charge to seek an amicable resolution of a dispute. [The details of the competent mediator will be specified here.]
sales.lawText: These terms are governed by French law. In the event of a dispute, and failing an amicable settlement, the French courts shall have jurisdiction. Users residing in the European Union may also use the European Commission's online dispute resolution platform.
